elements.sent-2411-001: The aim of this paper is to study the product of $n$ linear forms over function fields.
elements.sent-2411-002: We calculate the maximum value of the minima of the forms with determinant one when $n$ is small.
elements.sent-2411-003: The value is equal to the natural bound given by algebraic number theory.
elements.sent-2411-004: Our proof is based on a reduction theory of diagonal group orbits on homogeneous spaces.
elements.sent-2411-005: We also show that the forms defined algebraically correspond to periodic orbits with respect to the diagonal group actions.
elements.sent-2411-006: The study of the product of linear forms with real coefficients has a long history, see e.g.~\cite{Davenport38}\cite{Rogers50}\cite{Cassels55 .}
elements.sent-2411-007: Some challenging open questions remain unanswered, such as Littlewood conjecture and Cassels-Swinnerton-Dyer-Margulis conjecture.
elements.sent-2411-008: In recent years the dynamics of diagonal group actions on homogeneous spaces plays an important role, see e.g.~\cite{LW and \cite{EKL .}}
elements.sent-2411-009: The aim of this paper is to study the product of linear forms with coefficients in the field $\K=\F((t^{-1 ))$ of formal Laurent series over a finite field $\F$.}
elements.sent-2411-010: These forms are related to the diagonal group actions on the homogeneous space $G/\Gamma$ where $G=\SL_n(\K)$ and $\Gamma=\SL_n(\F)$.
elements.sent-2411-011: Besides its own interests, we hope to give some insights to the classical questions.
elements.sent-2411-012: Suppose the field $\F$ has $q=p^e$ elements where $p$ is a prime number and $e$ is a positive integer.
elements.sent-2411-013: We endow $\K$ with an ultrametric norm $|\cdot|$ so that $|bt^n|=q^n$ for $b\in \F\setminus\{ 0\}, n\in \Z$.
elements.sent-2411-014: It is the completion of the rational function field $\F(t)$ at $\infty$.
elements.sent-2411-015: Let $L(\bx)=l_1(\bx) \cdots l_n(\bx)$ be a nondegenerate product of linear forms in $n$-variables ($n\ge 2$), i.e.~$l_1, \ldots, l_n$ are linearly independent linear forms on $\K^n$.
elements.sent-2411-016: It is clear that $L$ is nondegenerate if and only if it has nonzero determinant $\det(L)$ which is the determinant of the matrix $g=(a_{ij )$ if $l_i(\bx)=a_{i1 x_1+ \cdots + a_{in x_n$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: In this way, an $n$-by-$n$ matrix $g$ gives a product of linear forms $L_g$.
elements.sent-2411-018: This gives a parameterization of products of linear forms by matrices.
elements.sent-2411-019: We are trying to understand the values of $L$ at $\bx\in \F^n$.
elements.sent-2411-020: Since $(aL)(\bx)=a\cdot L(\bx) $ for $a\in \K$, it suffices to understand the values of forms which have determinant $1$.
elements.sent-2411-021: These forms are parameterized by elements of $G$.
elements.sent-2411-022: The minimal norm of values of $L$ is denoted by \begin{equation}\bN (L)=\inf \{ |L(\bx)|: \bx\in \F^n, \bx \neq 0 \}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-023: Let $\mathfrak m_n$ be the infimum of the real numbers in the set \begin{equation}\mathfrak M_n=\{ \bN(L_g)^{-1}: g\in G\}=\{ \bN(L_g)^{-1} |\det (g)|: g\in \GL_n(\K)\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-024: In this paper we compute $\mathfrak m_n$ when $\frac{n { q}$ is small.}
elements.sent-2411-025: The value of $\mathfrak m_n$ can be interpreted algebraically as the minimal absolute value of discriminants of certain degree $n$ extensions of $\F(t)$.
elements.sent-2411-026: Recall that for a finite separable extension $k$ of degree $n$ over $\F(t)$ the discriminant of $k/\F(t)$ is \begin{equation}d_k=\det (Tr_{k/\F(t)}(\alpha_i\alpha_j))\end{equation} where $\alpha_1,\ldots , \alpha_n$ is an $\F$-basis of the integral closure of $\F$ in $k$.
elements.sent-2411-027: We remark here that $d_k$ is well-defined up to multiplications by elements of $\{c^2: 0\neq c\in \F\}$.
elements.sent-2411-028: This ambigurity will not affect its usage and is more convenient for our purposes.
elements.sent-2411-029: Moreover, we say $k$ is split at $\infty$ if $k=\F(t, \alpha)$ and the minimal polynomial of $\alpha$ over $\F(t)$ has $n$-distinct roots in $\K$.
elements.sent-2411-030: This is equivalent to that there are $n$-distinct $\F(t)$-embeddings of $k$ into $\K$, say $\sigma_1, \ldots, \sigma_n$.
elements.sent-2411-031: For the \begin{equation}\label{eq;rational} L(\bx)=\prod_{i=1}^n \sigma_i(\alpha_1) x_1+\sigma_i(\alpha_2)x_2+\cdots+ \sigma_i(\alpha_n) x_n,\end{equation} one has $\bN (L)=1$ and $\det (L)= |d_k|^{1/2}$.
elements.sent-2411-032: Therefore, $|d_k|^{1/2}\in \mathfrak M_n$ for all degree $n$ extensions $k$ of $\F(t)$ split at $\infty$.
elements.sent-2411-033: We use $disc-\mathfrak{m _n$ to denote the minimum of these $|d_k|^{1/2}$.}
elements.sent-2411-034: Since $\mathfrak m_n $ is the infimum of values in $\mathfrak M_n$, we have \begin{equation}\label{eq;upper} \mathfrak m_n \le disc-m_n.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-035: For those integers $n$ in Theorem \ref{thm;main we actually have equality.}
elements.sent-2411-036: A field $k$ split at infinity corresponds to a totally real extension of $\Q$.
elements.sent-2411-037: In the classical case it is known that $\mathfrak m_2 =\sqrt 5=disc-\mathfrak{m _2$ by Hurwitz \cite{Hurwitz and $\mathfrak m_3 =7=disc-\mathfrak{m _3$ by Davenport \cite{Davenport38 .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-038: Even for $n=4$, the precise value of $\mathfrak m_4 $ is not known.
elements.sent-2411-039: Theorem \ref{thm;compare gives more evidence that equality might hold in (\ref{eq;upper ) for both real and function field cases.}}
elements.sent-2411-040: We estimate $\mathfrak m_n$ from below and above by different methods.
elements.sent-2411-041: For the lower bound we develop a reduction theory for $A$-orbits of $G/\Gamma$, where $A$ be subgroup of $G$ consisting of diagonal matrices.
elements.sent-2411-042: We state the reduction as a decomposition of the group $G$.
elements.sent-2411-043: Let $\mathfrak{p =\{\alpha\in\K: |\alpha|<1\}$ be the unique maximal ideal of the valuation ring $\mathfrak{o}$ $=\{\alpha\in\K:|\alpha|\leq 1\}$ of $\K$.}
elements.sent-2411-044: The analogue of Theorem \ref{thm:AKT in the real case is called DOTU-decomposition and it does not hold when $n\ge 64$, see \cite[p.~624]{gruber and the references there.}}
elements.sent-2411-045: We use Theorem \ref{thm:AKT to give a lower bound of $\mathfrak m_n$ for all $n$ and estimate asymptotically from below.}
elements.sent-2411-047: After reviewing some basic facts of function fields, we reduce the calculation of $disc-\mathfrak m_n$ to that of the minimal genus of degree $n$ extensions of $\F(t)$ split at $\infty $.
elements.sent-2411-048: Then we calculate precisely when the minimal genus is equal to $0$ or $1$ using results on counting rational points of curves.
elements.sent-2411-049: This is the main reason that we have precise values of $\mathfrak m_n$ for small $n$.
elements.sent-2411-050: We will not study this direction in details to avoid technical complexity.
elements.sent-2411-051: We say $L$ has bounded type if $\bN (L)>0$.
elements.sent-2411-052: If $L$ is proportional to a form in (\ref{eq;rational ) with $\alpha_1, \ldots, \alpha_n$ an $\F(t)$-basis of $k$, then $L$ has bounded type.}
elements.sent-2411-053: For $n\ge 3$, it is an open question whether the converse holds.
elements.sent-2411-054: The forms in (\ref{eq;rational ) also correspond to periodic $A$-orbits in the finite volume homogeneous space $G/\Gamma$.}
elements.sent-2411-055: Here an orbit $Ag\Gamma$ is periodic in $G/\Gamma$ means $Stab_G(g\Gamma)$ is a lattice of $A$, which is equivalent to saying that $Ag\Gamma$ is compact.
elements.sent-2411-057: Theorem \ref{thm;equivalent allows one to use homogeneous dynamics to study isolation of rational forms as in \cite{LW .}}
elements.sent-2411-058: Other related questions were studied recently by several authors, see e.g.~\cite{Erez}\cite{Aranov}\cite{Paulin .}
elements.section-2411-002: Geometry of numbers over function fields
elements.sent-2411-059: We develop a reduction theory in Proposition \ref{lem;good basis which gives the decomposition of $G$ in Theorem \ref{thm:AKT .}}
elements.sent-2411-060: This allows us to give a lower bound of $\mathfrak m_n$.
elements.sent-2411-061: We write $R=\F$ to simplify the notation and fix $n\ge 2$.
elements.sent-2411-062: We consider $\K^n$ as column vectors over $\K$ and endow a norm $\|\cdot\|$ on it defined by \begin{equation}\Vert (v_1,v_2,\ldots,v_n)^{tr}\Vert= \max\{|v_1|, |v_2|,\ldots,|v_n|\}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-063: The geometry of numbers over function fields considers discrete and cocompact $R$-modules $\Lambda$ in $\K^n$.
elements.sent-2411-064: Since $R$ is a principle ideal domain we know that all these $\Lambda$ are free $R$-modules of rank $n$.
elements.sent-2411-065: Let $B_r$ be the closed ball of radius $r$ in $\K^n$, i.e. \begin{equation}B_r=\{\bv\in \K^n : \|\bv\|\le r \}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-066: For $1\le i\le n$, the $i$-th minimum of $\Lambda$ is \begin{equation}\lambda_i(\Lambda) =\inf\{r>0: \dimspan_\K (\Lambda \cap B_r)\ge i \}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-067: Let $\Lambda$ be a discrete and cocompact $R$-module in $\K^n$.

elements.sent-2411-269: Recall that a valuation (or place) of $k$ is a surjective homomorphism $v: k^* \to \Z$ such that $v(x+y)\ge \inf \{ v(x), v(y) \}$.

elements.sent-2411-275: The residue field $F(v)$ of $v$ is the quotient of the valuation ring $\mathfrak o_v:=\{x\in k_v: v(x)\ge 0 \}$ by its maximal ideal.
elements.sent-2411-276: Let $\mathcal{P _k$ be the set of valuations of $k$.}
